revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 長浜神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|250px|長浜神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 島根県出雲市西園町上長浜4258 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46|N|132|40|53|E|}} |祭神 = 八束水臣津野命 |社格 = 県社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = 妙見神社 |例祭 = 10月13日 |神事 = }} '''長浜神社'''は[[島根県]][[出雲市]]西園町にある[[神社]]である。出雲國神仏霊場第19番。 島根半島の西の付け根の辺り、『[[出雲国風土記]]』所載の「薗之長濱」、もしくは「薗松山」に比定される砂丘地帯の近くにある[[妙見山]]に鎮座する。スポーツ、勝利の神として信仰を集める。 == 祭神 == [[国引き神話]]の主人公である八束水臣津野命（やつかみずおみつの）を主祭神とし、布帝耳命（ふてみみ）・淤美豆奴命（おみずぬ）を配神として祀る。 布帝耳命・淤美豆奴命は『[[古事記]]』の[[スサノオ]]の系譜に名前のみ登場する神である。淤美豆奴命と布帝耳命は系譜では夫婦となっているが、ここでは淤美豆奴命は八束水臣津野命の子ということになっている。 == 歴史 == 『出雲国風土記』[[出雲郡]]条に記載の「出雲社」、[[延喜式神名帳]]記載の「出雲神社」に比定する説がある[[千家俊信]]『出雲国式社考』など。しかし長浜神社の鎮座する地域は明らかに[[神門郡]]の領域である。 中世から近世にかけては「妙見神社」「薗村妙見神社」などと呼ばれた。[[明治5年]]（1872年）に現在の社名となった。 [[延宝]]3年（[[1675年]]）に社殿が焼失し、[[松江藩]]主[[松平綱近]]の命によって造営された。 == 境内 == *坂下荒神社 *稲荷神社 *天神社 *春日神社 *秋葉神社 *要石・夫婦石・岐神社・荒神社・三輪鳥居 *弓掛けの松 == 行事 == 例祭日は10月13日である。毎年10月の上～中旬の日曜日には国引き神話にちなんだジャンボ綱引き大会が行われる。 == 参考文献 == *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』　1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 関連項目 == *[[金持神社]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html 長浜神社（出雲國神仏霊場）] *[http://www.izumo.ne.jp/~nagahama-jinja/ 長浜神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはましんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:出雲市]]

{{神社 |名称 = 長浜神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|250px|長浜神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 島根県出雲市西園町上長浜4258 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46|N|132|40|53|E|region:JP-32_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 八束水臣津野命 |社格 = 県社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = 妙見神社 |札所等 = [[出雲國神仏霊場]]19番 |例祭 = [[10月13日]] |神事 = }} '''長浜神社'''は[[島根県]][[出雲市]]西園町にある[[神社]]である。[[出雲國神仏霊場]]第19番。 島根半島の西の付け根の辺り、『[[出雲国風土記]]』所載の「薗之長濱」、もしくは「薗松山」に比定される砂丘地帯の近くにある[[妙見山]]に鎮座する。スポーツ、勝利の神として信仰を集める。 == 祭神 == [[国引き神話]]の主人公である八束水臣津野命（やつかみずおみつの）を主祭神とし、布帝耳命（ふてみみ）・淤美豆奴命（おみずぬ）を配神として祀る。 布帝耳命・淤美豆奴命は『[[古事記]]』の[[スサノオ]]の系譜に名前のみ登場する神である。その系譜では淤美豆奴命と布帝耳命は夫婦となっているが、この神社では淤美豆奴命は八束水臣津野命の子ということになっている。 == 歴史 == 『出雲国風土記』[[出雲郡]]条に記載の「出雲社」、[[延喜式神名帳]]記載の「出雲神社」に比定する説がある[[千家俊信]]『出雲国式社考』など。しかし長浜神社の鎮座する地域は明らかに[[神門郡]]の領域である。 中世から近世にかけては「妙見社」「薗村妙見神社」などと呼ばれた。[[明治5年]]（1872年）に現在の社名となった。 [[延宝]]3年（[[1675年]]）に社殿が焼失し、[[松江藩]]主[[松平綱近]]の命によって造営された。 == 境内 == *坂下荒神社 *稲荷神社 *天神社 *春日神社 *秋葉神社 *要石・夫婦石・岐神社・荒神社・三輪鳥居 *弓掛けの松 == 行事 == 例祭日は10月13日である。毎年10月の上～中旬の日曜日には国引き神話にちなんだジャンボ綱引き大会が行われる。 == 参考文献 == *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』　1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 関連項目 == *[[金持神社]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html 長浜神社（出雲國神仏霊場）] *[http://www.izumo.ne.jp/~nagahama-jinja/ 長浜神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはましんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:出雲市]]

{{神社 |名称 = 長浜神社(ながはまじんじゃ) |画像 = [[ファイル:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|250px|長浜神社拝殿]]

{{神社 |名称 = 長浜神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|250px|長浜神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 島根県出雲市西園町上長浜4258 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46|N|132|40|53|E|region:JP-32_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 八束水臣津野命 |社格 = 県社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = 妙見神社 |札所等 = [[出雲國神仏霊場]]19番 |例祭 = [[10月13日]] |神事 = }} '''長浜神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[出雲市]]西園町にある[[神社]]である。[[出雲國神仏霊場]]第19番。 島根半島の西の付け根の辺り、『[[出雲国風土記]]』所載の「薗之長濱」、もしくは「薗松山」に比定される砂丘地帯の近くにある[[妙見山]]に鎮座する。スポーツ、勝利の神として信仰を集める。 == 祭神 == [[国引き神話]]の主人公である八束水臣津野命（やつかみずおみつの）を主祭神とし、布帝耳命（ふてみみ）・淤美豆奴命（おみずぬ）を配神として祀る。 布帝耳命・淤美豆奴命は『[[古事記]]』の[[スサノオ]]の系譜に名前のみ登場する神である。その系譜では淤美豆奴命と布帝耳命は夫婦となっているが、この神社では淤美豆奴命は八束水臣津野命の子ということになっている。 == 歴史 == 『出雲国風土記』[[出雲郡]]条に記載の「出雲社」、[[延喜式神名帳]]記載の「出雲神社」に比定する説がある[[千家俊信]]『出雲国式社考』など。しかし長浜神社の鎮座する地域は明らかに[[神門郡]]の領域である。 中世から近世にかけては「妙見社」「薗村妙見神社」などと呼ばれた。[[明治5年]]（1872年）に現在の社名となった。 [[延宝]]3年（[[1675年]]）に社殿が焼失し、[[松江藩]]主[[松平綱近]]の命によって造営された。 == 境内 == *坂下荒神社 *稲荷神社 *天神社 *春日神社 *秋葉神社 *要石・夫婦石・岐神社・荒神社・三輪鳥居 *弓掛けの松 == 行事 == 例祭日は10月13日である。毎年10月の上～中旬の日曜日には国引き神話にちなんだジャンボ綱引き大会が行われる。 == 参考文献 == *関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 *白井永二・土岐昌訓編『新装普及版 神社辞典』　1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X *瀧音能之『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 関連項目 == *[[金持神社]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html 長浜神社（出雲國神仏霊場）] *[http://www.izumo.ne.jp/~nagahama-jinja/ 長浜神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはましんしや}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]]

{{Otheruses||大分県大分市長浜町に鎮座する神社|長浜神社 (大分市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 長浜神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|250px|長浜神社拝殿]]

{{Otheruses||大分県大分市長浜町に鎮座する神社|長浜神社 (大分市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 長浜神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|250px|長浜神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 島根県出雲市西園町上長浜4258 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46|N|132|40|53|E|region:JP-32_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 八束水臣津野命 |社格 = 県社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = 妙見神社 |札所等 = [[出雲國神仏霊場]]19番 |例祭 = [[10月13日]] |神事 = }} '''長浜神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[出雲市]][[西園町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[出雲國神仏霊場]]第19番。 島根半島の西の付け根の辺り、『[[出雲国風土記]]』所載の「薗之長濱」、もしくは「薗松山」に比定される砂丘地帯の近くにある[[妙見山]]に鎮座する。スポーツ、勝利の神として信仰を集める。 == 祭神 == [[国引き神話]]の主人公である八束水臣津野命（やつかみずおみずぬ）を主祭神とし、[[布帝耳神]]（ふてみみ）・[[淤美豆奴神]]（おみずぬ）を配神として祀る。 布帝耳命・淤美豆奴命は『[[古事記]]』の[[スサノオ]]の系譜に名前のみ登場する神である。その系譜では淤美豆奴命と布帝耳命は夫婦となっているが、この神社では淤美豆奴命は八束水臣津野命の子ということになっている。一方八束水臣津野命と淤美豆奴神は同一神とする説もある。 == 歴史 == 『出雲国風土記』[[出雲郡]]条に記載の「出雲社」、[[延喜式神名帳]]記載の「出雲神社」に比定する説がある[[千家俊信]]『出雲国式社考』など。しかし長浜神社の鎮座する地域は明らかに[[神門郡]]の領域である。 中世から近世にかけては「妙見社」「薗村妙見神社」などと呼ばれた。[[明治5年]]（1872年）に現在の社名となった。 [[延宝]]3年（[[1675年]]）に社殿が焼失し、[[松江藩]]主[[松平綱近]]の命によって造営された。 == 境内 == *坂下荒神社 *稲荷神社 *天神社 *春日神社 *秋葉神社 *要石・夫婦石・岐神社・荒神社・三輪鳥居 *弓掛けの松 == 行事 == 例祭日は10月13日である。毎年10月の上～中旬の日曜日には国引き神話にちなんだジャンボ綱引き大会が行われる。 == 参考文献 == * 関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * 白井永二・[[土岐昌訓]] 編『新装普及版 神社辞典』　1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X * [[瀧音能之]]『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 関連項目 == * [[金持神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html 長浜神社（出雲國神仏霊場）] * [http://www.izumo.ne.jp/~nagahama-jinja/ 長浜神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはましんしや}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:出雲國神仏霊場]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=島根県}}

{{Otheruses||大分県大分市長浜町に鎮座する神社|長浜神社 (大分市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 長浜神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|250px|長浜神社拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 島根県出雲市西園町上長浜4258 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|46|N|132|40|53|E|region:JP-32_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 八束水臣津野命 |社格 = 県社 |創建 = |本殿 = 大社造 |別名 = 妙見神社 |札所等 = [[出雲國神仏霊場]]19番 |例祭 = [[10月13日]] |神事 = }} '''長浜神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ）は[[島根県]][[出雲市]][[西園町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[出雲國神仏霊場]]第19番。 島根半島の西の付け根の辺り、『[[出雲国風土記]]』所載の「薗之長濱」、もしくは「薗松山」に比定される砂丘地帯の近くにある[[妙見山]]に鎮座する。スポーツ、勝利の神として信仰を集める。 == 祭神 == [[国引き神話]]の主人公である八束水臣津野命（やつかみずおみずぬ）を主祭神とし、[[布帝耳神]]（ふてみみ）・[[淤美豆奴神]]（おみずぬ）を配神として祀る。 布帝耳命・淤美豆奴命は『[[古事記]]』の[[スサノオ]]の系譜に名前のみ登場する神である。その系譜では淤美豆奴命と布帝耳命は夫婦となっているが、この神社では淤美豆奴命は八束水臣津野命の子ということになっている。一方八束水臣津野命と淤美豆奴神は同一神とする説もある。 == 歴史 == 『出雲国風土記』[[出雲郡]]条に記載の「出雲社」、[[延喜式神名帳]]記載の「出雲神社」に比定する説がある[[千家俊信]]『出雲国式社考』など。しかし長浜神社の鎮座する地域は明らかに[[神門郡]]の領域である。 中世から近世にかけては「妙見社」「薗村妙見神社」などと呼ばれた。[[明治5年]]（1872年）に現在の社名となった。 [[延宝]]3年（[[1675年]]）に社殿が焼失し、[[松江藩]]主[[松平綱近]]の命によって造営された。 == 境内 == *坂下荒神社 *稲荷神社 *天神社 *春日神社 *秋葉神社 *要石・夫婦石・岐神社・荒神社・三輪鳥居 *弓掛けの松 == 行事 == 例祭日は10月13日である。毎年10月の上～中旬の日曜日には国引き神話にちなんだジャンボ綱引き大会が行われる。 == 参考文献 == * 関和彦『『出雲国風土記』註論』 2006年 [[明石書店]] ISBN 4-7503-2376-4 * 白井永二・[[土岐昌訓]] 編『新装普及版 神社辞典』　1997年 [[東京堂出版]] ISBN 4-490-10474-X * [[瀧音能之]]『古代の出雲事典』 2001年 [[新人物往来社]] ISBN 4-404-02941-1 == 関連項目 == * [[金持神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html 長浜神社（出雲國神仏霊場）] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ 長浜神社　公式サイト] {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはましんしや}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:出雲國神仏霊場]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=島根県}}

revision.text:
{{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Shrine |Name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) |Image = [[File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Nagahama Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}{{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |OtherNames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13th|ja|10月13日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

{{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Shrine |Name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) |Image = [[File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Nagahama Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}{{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |OtherNames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13th|ja|10月13日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:長浜神社]]

During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:長浜神社]]

{{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Shrine |Name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) |image = [[File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Nagahama Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}{{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |OtherNames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13th|ja|10月13日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}{{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |OtherNames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13th|ja|10月13日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

{{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Shrine |Name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) |image = [[File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Nagahama Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}{{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |OtherNames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13th|ja|10月13日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

{{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Shrine |name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Nagahama Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}{{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Othernames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13th|ja|10月13日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

{{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Nagahama Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}{{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Othernames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13th|ja|10月13日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

{{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Nagahama Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}{{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Othernames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13th|ja|10月13日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo]] [[Category:Izumo Province Sacred Sites]] [[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:長浜神社]]

During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Nagahama Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}{{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Othernames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13th|ja|10月13日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}{{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Othernames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13th|ja|10月13日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}{{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Othernames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13th|ja|10月13日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site] During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * revision.comment: Text replacement - "出雲国" to "Izumo Province "
revision.text:
{{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG|270px|Nagahama Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}{{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Othernames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13th|ja|10月13日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Izumo City|ja|出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場}} pilgrimage. Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Myōken Mountain|ja|妙見山}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings. == Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami. Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity. == History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡}} section of the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others. However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡}}. During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|ja|明治5年}}. In {{ill|1675|ja|延宝3年}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|ja|松江藩}}. == Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows == Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology. == References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanemochi Shrine|ja|金持神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site]
