'''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Izumo, Shimane|Izumo City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395860}} pilgrimage.
Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine rests on [[:en:Mount Myōken|Myōken Mountain]] at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings.
== Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunibiki Myth|1=Kunibiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420924}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami.
Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity.
== History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|1=Izumo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10896679}} section of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.<ref>[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others.</ref> However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591020}}.
During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in [[:en:1872|Meiji 5]].
In [[:en:1675|1675]], the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Tsunachika|1=Matsudaira Tsunachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530561}}, lord of the [[:en:Matsue Domain|Matsue Domain]].
== Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows
== Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology.
== References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akashi Shoten|1=Akashi Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512528}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501312}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1.
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Kamochi Shrine|Kanemochi Shrine]]
== External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site]
{{translated page|ja|長浜神社|version=102199201|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows
== Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology.
== References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akashi Shoten|1=Akashi Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512528}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501312}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1.
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamochi Shrine|en|Kamochi Shrine|WD=Q246463|lt=Kanemochi Shrine}}
== External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nagahama-jinja]][[ja:長浜神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nagahama-jinja]][[ja:長浜神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nagahama-jinja]][[ja:長浜神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11653315]] Wikidata name: Nagahama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34597222222222|N|132.68102777777779|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|en|Taisha-zukuri|WD=Q387874|lt=Taisha-zukuri}} |Othernames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395860}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13|en|October 13|WD=Q2925|lt=October 13th}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166}} }}
'''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395860}} pilgrimage.
Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Mount Myōken|en|Mount Myōken|WD=Q868348|lt=Myōken Mountain}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings.
== Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunibiki Myth|1=Kunibiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420924}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami.
Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity.
== History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|1=Izumo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10896679}} section of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.<ref>[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others.</ref> However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591020}}.
During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5}}.
In {{ill|1675|en|1675|WD=Q7624|lt=1675}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Tsunachika|1=Matsudaira Tsunachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530561}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|en|Matsue Domain|WD=Q1017072|lt=Matsue Domain}}.
== Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows
== Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology.
== References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akashi Shoten|1=Akashi Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512528}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501312}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1.
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamochi Shrine|en|Kamochi Shrine|WD=Q246463|lt=Kanemochi Shrine}}
== External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site]

The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings.

Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity.
== History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|1=Izumo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10896679}} section of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.<ref>[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others.</ref> However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591020}}.
During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5}}.
In {{ill|1675|en|1675|WD=Q7624|lt=1675}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Tsunachika|1=Matsudaira Tsunachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530561}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|en|Matsue Domain|WD=Q1017072|lt=Matsue Domain}}.
== Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows
== Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology.
== References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akashi Shoten|1=Akashi Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512528}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501312}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1.
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamochi Shrine|en|Kamochi Shrine|WD=Q246463|lt=Kanemochi Shrine}}
== External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site]

Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Mount Myōken|en|Mount Myōken|WD=Q868348|lt=Myōken Mountain}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. {{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34597222222222|N|132.68102777777779|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|en|Taisha-zukuri|WD=Q387874|lt=Taisha-zukuri}} |Othernames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395860}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13|en|October 13|WD=Q2925|lt=October 13th}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166}} }}
'''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395860}} pilgrimage.
Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Mount Myōken|en|Mount Myōken|WD=Q868348|lt=Myōken Mountain}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings.
== Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunibiki Myth|1=Kunibiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420924}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami.
Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity.
== History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|1=Izumo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10896679}} section of the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.<ref>[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others.</ref> However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591020}}.
During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5}}.
In {{ill|1675|en|1675|WD=Q7624|lt=1675}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Tsunachika|1=Matsudaira Tsunachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530561}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|en|Matsue Domain|WD=Q1017072|lt=Matsue Domain}}.
== Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows
== Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology.
== References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akashi Shoten|1=Akashi Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512528}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501312}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1.
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamochi Shrine|en|Kamochi Shrine|WD=Q246463|lt=Kanemochi Shrine}}
== External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site]
{{translated page|ja|長浜神社|version=102199201|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|1=Izumo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10896679}} section of the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.<ref>[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others.</ref> However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591020}}.
During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}}.
In {{ill|1675|en|1675|WD=Q7624|lt=1675|1=1675}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Tsunachika|1=Matsudaira Tsunachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530561}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|en|Matsue Domain|WD=Q1017072|lt=Matsue Domain|1=Matsue Domain}}.
== Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows
== Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology.
== References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akashi Shoten|1=Akashi Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512528}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501312}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1.
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}

{{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City|1=Izumo, Shimane}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34597222222222|N|132.68102777777779|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|en|Taisha-zukuri|WD=Q387874|lt=Taisha-zukuri|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |Othernames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395860}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13|en|October 13|WD=Q2925|lt=October 13th|1=October 13}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} }}
'''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395860}} pilgrimage.
Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Mount Myōken|en|Mount Myōken|WD=Q868348|lt=Myōken Mountain|1=Mount Myōken}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings.
== Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunibiki Myth|1=Kunibiki-shinwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420924}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami.
Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity.
== History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|1=Izumo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10896679}} section of the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.<ref>[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others.</ref> However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591020}}.
During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}}.
In {{ill|1675|en|1675|WD=Q7624|lt=1675|1=1675}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Tsunachika|1=Matsudaira Tsunachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530561}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|en|Matsue Domain|WD=Q1017072|lt=Matsue Domain|1=Matsue Domain}}.
== Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows
== Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology.

{{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City|1=Izumo, Shimane}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34597222222222|N|132.68102777777779|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|en|Taisha-zukuri|WD=Q387874|lt=Taisha-zukuri|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |Othernames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395860}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13|en|October 13|WD=Q2925|lt=October 13th|1=October 13}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} }}
'''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395860}} pilgrimage.
Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Mount Myōken|en|Mount Myōken|WD=Q868348|lt=Myōken Mountain|1=Mount Myōken}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings.
== Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunibiki Myth|1=Kunibiki-shinwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420924}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami.
Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity.
== History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|1=Izumo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10896679}} section of the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.<ref>[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others.</ref> However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591020}}.
During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}}.
In {{ill|1675|en|1675|WD=Q7624|lt=1675|1=1675}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Tsunachika|1=Matsudaira Tsunachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11530561}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|en|Matsue Domain|WD=Q1017072|lt=Matsue Domain|1=Matsue Domain}}.
== Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows
== Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology.
== References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akashi Shoten|1=Akashi Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512528}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501312}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1.
== Footnotes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kamochi Shrine|en|Kamochi Shrine|WD=Q246463|lt=Kanemochi Shrine|1=Kamochi Shrine}}
== External Links == * [http://www.shinbutsu.jp/50.html Nagahama Shrine (Izumo Sacred Sites)] * [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine Official Site]

== History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|1=Izumo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10896679}} section of the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.<ref>[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others.</ref> However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11591020}}.
During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}}.
In {{ill|1675|en|1675|qid=Q7624|lt=1675|1=1675}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Tsunachika|1=Matsudaira Tsunachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11530561}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|en|Matsue Domain|qid=Q1017072|lt=Matsue Domain|1=Matsue Domain}}.
== Grounds == * Sakasita Kōjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Akiha Shrine * Landmark stones (Kaname Stone, Meoto Stone), Kōjin Shrine, Mikumari Torii * Pine tree associated with bows
== Events == The annual festival is held on October 13. During the early to mid-October weekends, the "Kunibiki Myth Giant Tug-of-War" competition is hosted, inspired by the shrine's mythology.
== References == * Kazuhiro Seki, *Commentary on the Izumo Fudoki*, 2006, {{ill|Akashi Shoten|ja|明石書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akashi Shoten|1=Akashi Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512528}}, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Eiji Shirai & Masanori Toki, eds., *Expanded Dictionary of Shinto Shrines*, 1997, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524645}}, ISBN 4-490-10474-X. * Yoshiyuki Takinoto, *Ancient Izumo Encyclopedia*, 2001, {{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11501312}}, ISBN 4-404-02941-1.

{{Redirect|Nagahama Shrine (長濱神社)|Other|Nagahama Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nagahama Shrine (長浜神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nagahamajinjahaiden.JPG |location = {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|qid=Q825166|lt=Izumo City|1=Izumo, Shimane}} Nishizono Town, Kamina, Nagahama 4258 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.34597222222222|N|132.68102777777779|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto |Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Established = |MainHallStyle = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|en|Taisha-zukuri|qid=Q387874|lt=Taisha-zukuri|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |Othernames = Myōken Shrine |Pilgrimage = {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395860}} #19 |Festival = {{ill|October 13|en|October 13|qid=Q2925|lt=October 13th|1=October 13}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane|label=Nagahama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |location = {{ill|Izumo|qid=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} }}
'''Nagahama Shrine''' (長浜神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|qid=Q825166|lt=Izumo City|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, in the Nishizono area. It is listed as the 19th site of the {{ill|Izumo Province Sacred Sites|ja|出雲國神仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province Sacred Sites|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395860}} pilgrimage.
Situated near the sand dune region identified with "Sono no Nagahama" or "Sono Matsuyama" in the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine rests on {{ill|Mount Myōken|en|Mount Myōken|qid=Q868348|lt=Myōken Mountain|1=Mount Myōken}} at the western base of the Shimane Peninsula. The shrine is venerated as a place for sports and victory blessings.
== Deities == The main enshrined deity is Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto, the protagonist of the {{ill|Kunibiki Myth|ja|国引き神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunibiki Myth|1=Kunibiki-shinwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420924}}. The auxiliary deities are Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami.
Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami appear only by name in the genealogy of {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}. In this shrine's context, Futeimimi no Kami and Omizunu no Kami are considered spouses, while Omizunu no Kami is regarded as Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto's child. There is also a theory suggesting Yasakaomizuno no Mikoto and Omizunu no Kami are the same deity.
== History == The shrine is hypothesized to correspond to the "Izumo Shrine" in the {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|1=Izumo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10896679}} section of the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or the "Izumo Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.<ref>[[Senke Toshinobu]], *Izumo Province Shikishakō*, among others.</ref> However, the shrine's location falls clearly within the domain of {{ill|Kamado District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11591020}}.
During the medieval and early modern periods, the shrine was known as "Myōken Shrine" or "Sonovillage Myōken Shrine." The current name was adopted in {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5|1=1872}}.
In {{ill|1675|en|1675|qid=Q7624|lt=1675|1=1675}}, the shrine's buildings were destroyed by fire and rebuilt under the orders of {{ill|Matsudaira Tsunachika|ja|松平綱近|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Tsunachika|1=Matsudaira Tsunachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11530561}}, lord of the {{ill|Matsue Domain|en|Matsue Domain|qid=Q1017072|lt=Matsue Domain|1=Matsue Domain}}.
